title: Testing, Fault Injection, Release, and Production Defense · Runtime Contract: Fake Emulator Boundary
summary: A service passes mocked unit tests and a local emulator demo, then deploys new SDK and CDN settings without race, AWS contract, security, load, rollback, restore, or cost evidence. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the fake emulator boundary increment in production file-delivery defense dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Use fakes and emulators for deterministic feedback while listing unsupported AWS semantics and assigning controlled integration tests.
Use table, property, and fuzz tests for key, range, policy, lifecycle, state-machine, checksum, retry, and cache invariants with changed cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Fake Emulator Boundary · Theory Fake Emulator Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 3288 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate release on deterministic, Go runtime, controlled AWS, fault, load, restore, and rollback evidence; record cleanup, rollback, restoration, and residual-risk ownership; then change region, range, or signed header and explain the exact failure boundary. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use fakes and emulators for deterministic feedback while listing unsupported AWS semantics and assigning controlled integration tests.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Fake Emulator Boundary contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Bound Fake Emulator Boundary · Theory Fake Emulator Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 8145 during an SDK and infrastructure canary. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate release on deterministic, Go runtime, controlled AWS, fault, load, restore, and rollback evidence; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then double concurrent transfers and keep process resource limits intact. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use fakes and emulators for deterministic feedback while listing unsupported AWS semantics and assigning controlled integration tests.
